lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Code Tidy Sweep ]─ ┐

Code Tidy Sweep

Scan source repos against coding rules, flag violations, triage, fix. Uses parallel workers per repo.

Prerequisites

  • Source repos checked out locally (see docs/knowledge/environment/repo-relationships.md)
  • Coding rules: docs/context/rules/coding.md
  • Security rules: docs/context/rules/security.md

Scope

Active source repos only: lore, create-lore, lore-docs, lore-docker. Skip vendored deps, node_modules, generated files, test fixtures, and lock files.

Phase 1: Inventory (sequential)

Catalog what each repo contains so workers get balanced file sets.

For each repo:
  1. List source files (js, ts, sh, json, yml) with line counts
  2. Group by directory or concern
  3. If a repo has < 500 lines total, assign one worker
  4. If > 500 lines, split into 2 groups by directory

Output: a file-group assignment table for Phase 2.

Phase 2: Scan (parallel — sonnet workers)

Launch one sonnet worker per file group. Each worker loads:

  • docs/context/rules/coding.md
  • docs/context/rules/security.md
  • Its assigned file list

Each worker scans for:

Rule 2 — Write Less Code

  • Abstractions used only once (premature helpers, unnecessary wrappers)
  • Configurability or extensibility nobody uses
  • Error handling for impossible scenarios
  • Code that could be significantly shorter

Rule 3 — Change Only What You Must

  • Dead imports, unused variables, unreachable functions
  • Commented-out code left behind
  • Formatting-only changes mixed with logic changes (in recent commits)

Rule 4 — Prove It Works

  • Untested public functions or exported modules
  • Hardcoded secrets, API keys, tokens, connection strings
  • Unvalidated external input in queries or commands
  • Exposed internals (private state accessible from outside)

Security rules

  • Secrets in committed files (.env values, inline tokens, key material)
  • Auth tokens in URL query strings
  • String-concatenated queries with user input
  • Missing output escaping (HTML, shell, SQL contexts)

Worker return format

Each worker returns a violations list:

| File | Line | Rule | Severity | Description |

Severity levels:

  • security — secrets, injection vectors. Fix immediately.
  • violation — clear rule breach. Fix in Phase 4.
  • judgment — arguable. Operator decides.

Phase 3: Triage (sequential — orchestrator)

Orchestrator merges all worker reports and splits into three buckets:

  1. Security — fix now, no discussion
  2. Auto-fix — clear violations where the fix is obvious (dead code removal, unused imports)
  3. Review — judgment calls presented to operator with context

Present the review bucket to the operator. Mark approved items for Phase 4, defer the rest.

Phase 4: Fix (parallel — sonnet workers)

Launch workers to apply approved fixes. Split by repo (same grouping as Phase 2).

Each worker loads:

  • docs/context/rules/coding.md
  • docs/context/rules/security.md
  • The approved violations list for its file group
  • Relevant skills from .lore/skills/ (e.g., bash-macos-compat for shell scripts)

Rules:

  • One fix per violation. Don’t expand scope.
  • Run existing tests after each file change. If tests break, revert and flag.
  • Don’t add tests for pre-existing untested code — only flag it in the report.
  • Security fixes take priority and must not be deferred.

Phase 5: Verify (sequential)

After all workers complete:

# Run full test suites per repo
cd /path/to/lore && npm test
cd /path/to/create-lore && npm test

# Check for regressions
git diff --stat   # Review change magnitude per repo

# Confirm no secrets leaked
grep -rn 'password\|secret\|token\|api_key' --include='*.{js,ts,sh,json}' | grep -v node_modules

Review the combined diff before committing.

Model Allocation

PhaseModelWorkersRationale
ScanSonnet2–4Rule judgment needs semantic understanding; Haiku misses nuance
FixSonnet2–4Targeted edits within scoped file sets
Security scanSonnet(included in scan)Data flow tracing requires code comprehension
TriageOrchestrator1Human-in-the-loop; no delegation

Haiku is insufficient — detecting “over-engineering” or “speculative features” is a judgment call, not pattern matching. Opus adds cost without meaningful accuracy gain; the rules are concrete enough for Sonnet.

Parallelization Summary

Phase 2 (scan):     [repo-1 group-A] [repo-1 group-B] [repo-2] [repo-3]
                     ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 3 (triage):   [orchestrator merges + operator review]
                     ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 4 (fix):      [repo-1 group-A] [repo-1 group-B] [repo-2] [repo-3]
                     ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 5 (verify):   [sequential test runs + diff review]

Cadence

  • After major feature branches merge — code rules drift during long-lived branches
  • Before releases — catch violations before they ship
  • Quarterly — general hygiene alongside docs-health-sweep
  • Code-only: Skip docs-health-sweep Passes 1, 2a, 3 when docs are stable
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
